paper_title: Material and Methods of Foraminiferal Study

methods.description: Process for collecting and preparing extant and fossil foraminiferal samples for further analysis.

steps.description: Recent foraminiferal tests were collected from a beach at Ronsard Bay near Cervantes (Western Australia) except for Procerolagena gracilis which was selected from the Wojciech Majewski's collection from Admiralty Bay, King George Island, West Antarctica.
steps.searchable_summary: collect recent foraminiferal tests from specified locations

steps.description: Fossil material was derived from Bathonian black clays of the Ore-Bearing Częstochowa Clay Formation, which is well-exposed in the Częstochowa Region, central Poland.
steps.searchable_summary: collect fossil material from Częstochowa Clay Formation

steps.description: The examined specimens were coated with 4-10 nm of gold.
steps.searchable_summary: coat specimens with gold for SEM analysis

methods.description: Method to determine the mineralogy of Jurassic foraminiferal species using Raman spectroscopy to assess their state of preservation.

steps.description: Mineralogy of Jurassic supposedly aragonitic and calcitic species has been determined using a Raman spectroscopy at the Faculty of Chemistry, University of Warsaw.
steps.searchable_summary: perform Raman spectroscopy to determine mineralogy

steps.description: The analysis have been conducted with a dispersive Raman spectrometer coupled with an Olympus BX61 confocal microscope, using a diode-pumped, frequency-doubled Nd:YAG laser (532 nm, Pmax = 100 mW on the head).
steps.searchable_summary: use specific Raman spectrometer setup for analysis

steps.description: The spectrometer was equipped with a Peltier-cooled CCD detector (1024 × 256 pixels). The calibration standard used was the well-known 520 cm−1 Raman band of a silicon chip.
steps.searchable_summary: calibrate spectrometer using silicon chip standard

steps.description: This method allowed identification of calcium carbonate polymorphs (calcite and aragonite) by comparing obtained peak assignments with literature references.
steps.searchable_summary: identify calcium carbonate polymorphs by peak comparison
